title: NOC 41406 – Recreation, Sports and Fitness Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Recreation, Sports and Fitness Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers under NOC 41406 are professionals who design, evaluate, and administer sports, recreation, and fitness programs and policies across Canada. Classified under TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role supports public health promotion, community wellness initiatives, athletic program development, and recreational policy planning. These professionals work in government agencies, community centers, sports organizations, healthcare facilities, consulting firms, schools, and fitness institutions.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41406

duties: Provide expert advice on design and administration of sports and recreation programs
Assist organizations with program planning and public relations
Recreation, Sports and Fitness Policy Analysts
Conduct research and develop government policies
Establish standards for athletic safety and recreation programs
Recreation, Sports and Fitness Program Supervisors
Develop and coordinate recreation and fitness programs
employment_requirements: University degree in:Recreation administrationSports administrationPhysical educationKinesiologyRelated discipline
College diploma in recreation, sports, or fitness combined with relevant experience
Experience as recreation or fitness instructor for supervisory roles
Certification in specialized areas (fitness training, lifeguarding, coaching, etc.) may be required
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Recreation, Sports and Fitness Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers typically earn between:
$34,100 – $84,700 per year,depending on experience, specialization, and location.
Senior program managers and policy advisors may earn higher compensation.
job_outlook: Demand for safe and regulated athletic programming
The wellness and sports industry continues to grow across Canada.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 41406 are employed in:
Stable employment in public and community sectors
Growing demand in wellness and fitness planning
Career advancement into supervisory and policy roles
Eligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs
Opportunity to contribute to community health and recreation development
Professionals with experience in kinesiology, physical education, sports administration, or recreation planning are particularly competitive.
pathways: Professionals under this NOC may qualify for:
Education credentials and specialized certifications strengthen immigration competitiveness.
